questionHtml:
What is the heat change, in kJ, when 100.0 g of aluminium is heated from 19.0 °C to 32.0 °C?

Specific heat capacity of aluminium: 0.90 J g−1 K−1

A.  0.90×100.0×13.0

B.  0.90×100.0×286

C.  0.90×100.0×13.01000

D.  0.90×100.0×2861000

meta.title: What is the heat change, in kJ, when 100.0 g of aluminium is heated from 19.0 °C to 32.0 °C? Specific heat capacity of aluminium: 0.90 J g−1 K−1 A. 0.90×100.0×13.0 B. 0.90×100.0×28
breadcrumbLabels: Reactivity 1.1.4—The standard enthalpy change for a chemical reaction, ΔH⦵, refers to the heat transferred at constant pressure under standard conditions and states. It can be determined from the change in temperature of a pure substance. Apply the equations Q = mcΔT and ΔH = − Qn in the calculation of the enthalpy change of a reaction.
